problem: Native OpenXR and Spatial consumers need one neutral tracked-hand relation contract before mesh skinning, sampling, and appearance are divided among Matter, Lattice, and Optics.
owns: Handed tracked-joint relation snapshots with validity, confidence, reference space, and time.
Provider-neutral topology identity and mapping metadata references.
Cross-provider fixtures for missing, stale, and partially valid hands.
does_not_own: Mesh skinning, barycentric sampling, particle generation, or GPU buffers.
Hand material, wireframe appearance, or renderer composition.
Spatial SDK avatar internals or Quest app activation.
app_specific_exclusions: Mirror-X Spatial registration policy and debug marker layout.
Billboard flock counts, colors, controller shortcuts, and panel behavior.
Private recorded rig assets and visual acceptance screenshots.

provenance.source: Rusty Quest native OpenXR and Spatial hand-lab adapters
provenance.lesson: Share tracked relation and topology identities while keeping provider adapters and visual consumers separate.
provenance.rejected_overreach: Do not make Lattice own Matter skinning, Optics appearance, Spatial SDK resources, or app controls.

evidence: OpenXR-to-Spatial mapping fixtures already keep transforms explicit and reversible.
Spatial public APIs do not expose the built-in avatar hand topology.
The workflow feature lock disables the candidate by default.

rollback.strategy: Keep provider-specific app adapters and remove the shared contract adapter if cross-provider parity is not proven.
rollback.acceptance: Native and Spatial focused tests pass using their existing provider-specific paths with no shared contract dependency.
